rules_fired.because: Concentrated corrosive reagents (HCl, TFA, NaOH) release significant heat on dilution. Adding water to a concentrated corrosive can boil the liquid and cause spattering; always add the corrosive slowly to water. TFA and HCl volatilise in the hood — decant and dilute in a ventilated space. Neutralise to pH 6–8 before drain disposal per local regulations.
rules_fired.cite: Sigma-Aldrich HCl Safety Note 258148. ACS Safety in Academic Chemistry Laboratories, 7th ed., Ch. 4. EPA 40 CFR Part 261 (hazardous waste neutralisation).

rules_fired.because: TCEP (tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine) is a phosphine-based reducing agent that is stable to atmospheric oxygen in aqueous solution — unlike thiol reagents (DTT, β-mercaptoethanol) which oxidise within hours to days. TCEP solutions in standard buffer can be stored at 4°C for up to 30 days without significant loss of reducing activity. Single-use aliquots are recommended for irreplaceable stocks but are not obligatory for routine working solutions.
rules_fired.cite: Getz EB et al. (1999) Anal Biochem 273:73–80 (TCEP stability characterisation). Pierce TCEP·HCl Product Manual.
